feed.subtitle: A minimal way to show off something which I work on.

entry.title: BeagleBone GPIO
entry.summary: This post describes a BeagleBone GPIO project. About the project Hardware: BeagleBone board Feature: GPIO pin control Status: working project example

entry.title: Memory-Leak tool Valgrind
entry.summary: Understanding Valgrind Memory management is one of the most important parts of C and C++ programming. Unlike other higher-level languages, developers are responsible for manually allocating and freeing memory. This creates serious bugs if left unchecked. Some of these are as follows: Memory leaks Invalid memory access Use-after-free errors Uninitialized variable usage To help detec...

entry.title: CMake Passing Params
entry.summary: Passing Parameters from CMake to C/C++ Code One of the powerful features of CMake is the ability to pass configuration values directly into source code during build time. This is useful for: Build versioning Debug flags Feature toggles Platform-specific settings API endpoints Compile-time configurations Project Structure parameter-demo/ ├── CMakeLists.txt └── main.cpp E...

entry.title: CMake First Project
entry.summary: Creating Your First CMake Project Project Structure hello/ ├── CMakeLists.txt └── main.cpp Writing the Source Code main.cpp #include <iostream> int main() { std::cout << "Hello CMake Build!" << std::endl; return 0; } Creating the CMakeLists.txt File touch CMakeLists.txt CMakeLists.txt cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.10) project(Hello) add_executable(he...

entry.title: Process vs Threads
entry.summary: Linux Internals — Process vs Thread Process Definition A process is an independent running program with its own memory and resources. Features Separate address space Own PID Own heap, stack, globals Heavyweight Creation fork(); Example firefox vim gcc Thread Definition A thread is a lightweight execution unit inside a process. Features Shares process memory Own...
